summary: The build failed while trying to download a prebuilt nydus artifact with wget; the download returned a 502 error which caused the Docker build step to exit and the buildx bake to fail.
root_cause: A network/remote server error occurred when running wget to fetch the nydus release from GitHub; wget returned "HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error" and the RUN step exited with code 1, causing the build to fail.

evidence.quote: #72 0.149 wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: Shows wget received a 502 Bad Gateway/Proxy error when trying to download the artifact.

evidence.quote: #72 ERROR: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Indicates the RUN command that executed wget failed and exited with code 1, aborting the build step.

evidence.quote:
Dockerfile:336
--------------------
  334 |     ARG TARGETARCH
  335 |     SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
  336 | >>> RUN wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz
evidence.reason: Identifies the exact Dockerfile line that runs wget to fetch the nydus artifact.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Top-level CI error message showing buildx bake failed because the wget step did not complete successfully.

suggested_fix: Retry the job (transient network error). If failures persist, make the Dockerfile download step more robust (e.g., add retry logic or use curl/wget with --tries/--retry) or host the nydus artifact on a reliable mirror or vend the artifact into the build context so the build does not depend on the external GitHub release at build time.

summary: A functional test failed because pip could not download a test package from GitHub due to repeated HTTP 502 errors, causing the install command to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The test tests/functional/test_truststore.py::test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub] failed because pip could not fetch https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (ResponseError: 'too many 502 error responses'), resulting in an OSError and non-zero exit.

evidence.reason: Identifies the exact test that failed (appears in the failure lines).

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Shows pip failed to download the package from GitHub due to repeated 502 error responses, which is a network/transient error.
Shows the exact command the test ran that encountered the network error.
Demonstrates the test failed because the script/command exited with a non-zero return code.
The test is network-marked (the log shows the network marker), indicating it depends on external network access and is therefore susceptible to network failures.

suggested_fix: Rerun the CI job (this appears to be a transient network/502 error). If failures recur, make the test more resilient to external network errors by mocking or using a local test package server instead of downloading directly from GitHub, or add retry/backoff for the network fetch in the test harness.

summary: The CI job failed because one Jest test suite failed (test/config/get.ts), causing the pnpm test script to exit with a non-zero status.
root_cause: A test suite failed during Jest run: test/config/get.ts contains a failing test (error at test/config/get.ts:32:38) which caused the overall test command to exit with status 1.

evidence.quote: Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
evidence.reason: Shows that one test suite failed while others passed.
Stack location points to the failing test file and line number.

evidence.quote:
console.warn
      [WARN] Failed to replace env in config: ${ENV_VAR_123}
evidence.reason: A warning about missing environment variable substitution was emitted during tests, likely related to the failing config test.
The test script exited with status 1 indicating the failing test caused the process to return non-zero.

evidence.quote: Force exiting Jest: Have you considered using `--detectOpenHandles` to detect async operations that kept running after all tests finished?
evidence.reason: Jest forced exit after tests completed, indicating the run ended with a failure that terminated the job.

suggested_fix: Run the failing test locally (e.g. run the same jest command or run jest on test/config/get.ts) and inspect line 32 of test/config/get.ts. If the failure is due to missing environment replacement, ensure the test provides or mocks ENV_VAR_123 (or add the variable in the CI environment), or update the test to not rely on that environment variable.
